params.text:
Check for danger and response

Before you touch anyone, make sure the area is safe. This is the first step of the well-known DRABC approach — Danger, Response, Airway, Breathing, Compressions.

Danger

Look around. Is there anything that could harm you or the person — traffic, water, electricity, spilled fluids, an aggressive animal? You cannot help if you become a casualty yourself. Make the scene safe first.

Response

Kneel beside the person. Gently shake their shoulders and ask loudly, "Are you all right? Can you hear me?" If they respond, leave them in the position you found them (unless there is further danger), find out what is wrong, and get help if needed.

If there is no response, they are unresponsive. Shout for help straight away so that someone can assist you and fetch an AED — but do not leave the person yet.

params.question: You should always check the area is safe before approaching and helping a collapsed person.

l10n.wrongAnswerMessage: Not quite. Checking for danger comes first — you must not put yourself at risk, or you may end up as a second casualty.
l10n.correctAnswerMessage: Correct. Danger is the first step of DRABC. If you become injured yourself, you cannot help anyone.
